nativeRange.habitat: Slow-moving, vegetation-rich streams and rivers across central India and Malaya, with some references to the broader range extending west toward Iraq. Prefers planted, sheltered waters with abundant biofilm and leaf litter.

diet.notes: Excellent algae and biofilm grazers; also takes blanched vegetables (zucchini, cucumber, carrot, spinach), Indian almond / alder leaves, and standard shrimp pellets or sinking wafers. Mature, biofilm-rich tanks meet most of their nutritional needs, especially for fry. Feed sparingly to avoid fouling the water.

goodWith: small peaceful nano fish (chili rasboras, ember tetras, pygmy corydoras)
other dwarf shrimp (Amano, Bamboo, Tangerine Tiger, Malawa)
avoidWith: shrimp predators (most cichlids, gouramis, larger barbs)
Neocaridina species in breeding setups (no hybridisation but visually confusing colonies)

summary: Color-changing Caridina from India and Malaya, sold in green, blue, brown, and striped 'Malaya' or 'Indian Zebra' morphs. Generally regarded as tolerant of a wider parameter range than Crystal Shrimp, making it a good intermediate-friendly Caridina.
careNotes: Caridina cf. babaulti is a taxonomic complex of similar-looking shrimp distributed across central India through Malaya; the 'cf.' designation reflects unresolved formal classification. It is commonly traded under several names that may all be the same biological species in different color phases: Green Shrimp, Malaya Shrimp, Blue Malaya, Stripe Malaya, and Indian Zebra Shrimp. Individuals are known to change color rapidly in response to mood, substrate color, and lighting — a feature that distinguishes them from most Neocaridina morphs. Generally considered hardier and more parameter-tolerant than Crystal Red / Bee Caridina, but specimens are often reported as fragile on import and tend to do best in mature, biologically stable tanks (no ammonia or nitrite, nitrate ideally under 10 ppm). Like all dwarf shrimp, they are extremely sensitive to copper-bearing medications, fertilisers, and snail treatments. Source spread on water parameters is wide: temperatures reported between roughly 20-28 °C and pH between 6.2-8.0; the listed ranges (22-28 °C, pH 6.5-7.8) are averaged middle values that suit most retail stock. Will not hybridise with Neocaridina davidi morphs but can interbreed across babaulti color forms, so keep only one color morph per breeding tank if pure offspring are desired.
breedingNotes: Generally considered easy to moderate in mature, stable tanks. Females carry roughly 30-60 eggs (some reports up to 80) in clusters under the tail; eggs typically hatch in about 30 days, releasing fully-formed miniature shrimp with no planktonic larval stage. Fry are smaller than Neocaridina and rely heavily on biofilm and fine algae, so a well-established tank with mature substrate is recommended. Females are noticeably larger and rounder than males and usually show a visible saddle when carrying eggs. Sponge filters are advised to avoid intake of fry. Reported maturity around 75 days at roughly 2-2.5 cm. Does not crossbreed with Neocaridina species.

primary.name: The Shrimp Farm
primary.notes: Primary reference for general husbandry and parameter targets (pH 6.8-7.8, gH 4-14, temp 75-82 °F). Direct fetch returned 403 so content was reached via search-engine summaries and corroborating retailer pages.

additional.name: Flip Aquatics
additional.notes: Cross-check for water parameters (pH 7.0-7.6, gH 7-15, kH 2-8, TDS 150-350), tank size (4 gal minimum, 20 gal recommended), and Malaya morph identification. Notes Caridina babaulti is 'much hardier than a typical Caridina'. Temperature stated as 68-72 °F is unusually low vs other sources; averaged with consensus 75-82 °F to 22-28 °C in this entry.

additional.name: Shrimp and Snail Breeder (aquariumbreeder.com)
additional.notes: Cross-check for taxonomy (family Atyidae), native range (central India to Malaya, west to Iraq), size (2.5-4 cm), lifespan (1-2 years), color variants (Green / Stripes / Malaya / Brown / Red / Yellow / Blue), and breeding (30-day incubation, ~75-day maturity). Confirms color-change behavior and lack of Neocaridina hybridisation.

additional.name: Garnelio (EN)
additional.notes: Cross-check for temperature (24-28 °C), pH (7-7.5), size (~2.5 cm females), lifespan (18-24 months), and minimum tank 10-20 L. Confirms Malaysia as a native range and the white-striped Malaya color pattern.

additional.name: Aquatic Arts
additional.notes: Cross-check for copper sensitivity warning, breeding (30-50 eggs, ~30-day hatch), and Green color phase. Confirms maximum size of about 1.4 in (~3.5 cm).

crustacean.moltingNotes: Molts periodically as the shrimp grows; discarded exuviae are typically left in the tank as they re-mineralize the water and are sometimes eaten. Females usually molt just before mating, when they release pheromones that trigger a brief 'shrimp run' by males. Stable parameters (especially gH and kH) are important for clean molts; sudden swings can cause stuck molts.
crustacean.breedingNotes: Breeds readily in mature, stable freshwater tanks with abundant biofilm. ~30-day egg incubation, no planktonic phase; fry are miniature adults. Maintain single color morph per tank for pure offspring.
